title: Boechera johnstonii
taxonomy: Boechera johnstonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to North America and is a member of the genus Boechera. The genus Boechera is a group of plants that are often referred to as rockcresses. They are closely related to the genus Arabis and are sometimes considered to be a subset of that genus. Boechera johnstonii is further classified as a member of the species B. johnstonii, which is one of the many species within the Boechera genus.

intro: Boechera johnstonii, also known as Johnston's rockcress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Boechera johnstonii include isothiocyanates, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Boechera johnstonii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in capsule or tablet form to harness its medicinal properties.
